root_cause.text: The `EscapeCharacter.escape` method previously threw an exception when processing a `null` value instead of gracefully returning `null`, and the configured escape character could not be dynamically resolved within SpEL expressions.
root_cause.evidence: The patch replaces `Assert.notNull(value, "Value must be not null.");` with `if (value == null) { return null; }` in `EscapeCharacter.java` and adds an `escapeCharacter()` method to make the underlying character available to SpEL context.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The `EscapeCharacter.escape` method previously threw an exception when processing a `null` value instead of gracefully returning `null`, and the configured escape character could not be dynamically resolved within SpEL expressions.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch replaces `Assert.notNull(value, "Value must be not null.");` with `if (value == null) { return null; }` in `EscapeCharacter.java` and adds an `escapeCharacter()` method to make the underlying character available to SpEL context.

intent_record.intent_summary: Document the ability to configure the escape character via the `@EnableJpaRepositories` annotation.
intent_record.what: Added a single sentence to the documentation regarding the configuration of the escape character.
intent_record.how: Informs users that they can configure the escape character used for LIKE wildcards by setting the `escapeCharacter` attribute of the `@EnableJpaRepositories` annotation.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix method signature in documentation to match the actual SpEL escape method.
intent_record.what: Changed the method signature from escape(String, String) to escape(String) in the documentation.
intent_record.how: Updates the documentation to accurately reflect the correct method signature available in the SpEL context.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update documentation to reflect the usage of the configurable SpEL escape character method.
intent_record.what: Updated the `@Query` code example and surrounding documentation to use the single-argument `escape(String)` method and `escapeCharacter()` function instead of hardcoding the escape character.
intent_record.how: Ensures the documentation accurately reflects the new SpEL configuration for escaping wildcards in Spring Data JPA queries, using the globally configured escape character.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove unused Assert import to clean up code.
intent_record.what: Removed an unused import statement for org.springframework.util.Assert.
intent_record.how: This change has no impact on runtime behavior as it merely removes an unused dependency from the file.

intent_record.intent_summary: Allow null input values by returning null early instead of throwing an assertion exception.
intent_record.what: Replaced a not-null assertion with a conditional check that returns null if the input is null.
intent_record.how: Prevents runtime exceptions when null values are passed, allowing the program to handle them gracefully.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add getter method to expose the underlying character value.
intent_record.what: Added a new public method escapeCharacter() that returns the value field.
intent_record.how: Allows external components to access the previously encapsulated character value.

grouping_result.intent: Update documentation to reflect the usage of the configurable SpEL escape character method.
Fix method signature in documentation to match the actual SpEL escape method.
Remove unused Assert import to clean up code.
Allow null input values by returning null early instead of throwing an assertion exception.
Add getter method to expose the underlying character value.
Remove trailing whitespace in Javadoc comment.

consistency_review.core_intent: Update documentation to reflect the configuration and usage of the SpEL escape character.
Remove trailing whitespace in Javadoc comments.

relevance_review.reason: The group only contains documentation updates regarding the configuration and usage of the SpEL escape character, which does not directly address the code fix for the vulnerability root cause.
The group only contains a documentation change for a method signature, which does not address the core vulnerability logic regarding null value handling or dynamic resolution of escape characters.
The group only removes an unused import, which is a code cleanup change and does not directly address the vulnerability's root cause.
The group directly fixes the vulnerability by allowing null input values to gracefully return null instead of throwing an exception, which perfectly matches the root cause.
Adding a getter method to expose the underlying character value directly supports resolving the escape character within SpEL expressions, addressing a key aspect of the vulnerability root cause.
The group only contains cosmetic changes removing trailing whitespace in Javadoc comments, which is completely unrelated to the vulnerability involving null handling and SpEL expression resolution.
The group only removes an extraneous blank line, which is a minor formatting change unrelated to the logic of the vulnerability root cause.

candidates.rationale: This line enforces that the input value is not null and throws an exception if it is, which directly matches the vulnerability root cause.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement throws an exception when the input value is null, instead of gracefully returning null as described in the vulnerability root cause.
